# Project & Session Management (Web)

> **Implementation**: `jiuwenswarm/gateway/channel_manager/web/app_web_handlers.py` (handler registration), `jiuwenswarm/server/runtime/session/project_store.py` (project storage), `jiuwenswarm/server/runtime/session/session_metadata.py` (session metadata), `jiuwenswarm/server/runtime/session/session_rename.py` (rename shared logic)
> **中文版**: [../zh/项目与会话管理.md](../zh/项目与会话管理.md)

---

## 1. Overview

The Web channel provides project-scoped session management. A session is bound to a specific project via `project_id`, or uses the default empty project. Projects and sessions are linked via `project_id` matching.

### Core Concepts

| Concept | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **Default project** | Not persisted; dynamically injected by the API, `project_id="default"`. Rename/remove/pin are forbidden. Sessions with empty `project_id` or no matching visible project are assigned to the default project |
| **Soft delete** | `project.remove` marks `hidden:true` without deleting the record; recoverable. While hidden, its non-pinned sessions temporarily belong to the default project |
| **Pinned sessions** | Detached from project groups; fetched independently via `project.pinned_sessions`, sorted by `pin_order` ascending |
| **Pinned projects** | Visually distinguished from non-pinned projects; pinned projects appear first (sorted by `pin_order` ascending) |
| **project_id / project_dir immutability** | Once a session's `project_id` and `project_dir` are bound, they cannot be changed; no cross-project migration API is provided |

### Communication Convention

- Transport: WebSocket JSON messages
- Request structure: `{ "id": "<req_id>", "method": "<method_name>", "params": { ... } }`
- Response envelope (unified across all interfaces):

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `ok` | boolean | yes | Success flag |
| `payload` | object | on success | Response data |
| `error` | string | on failure | Error description |
| `code` | string | on failure | Error code |

### session_id Generation Convention

- `session_id` is **generated by the frontend**, format: `sess_<hex milliseconds timestamp>_<6-char hex random>`
- The `sess_` prefix is a Web convention: only sessions starting with `sess_` support `history.get` for history recovery
- The backend does not validate this format, but the frontend must follow it

---

## 2. Data Structures

### ProjectInfo

Returned by `project.list`.

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `project_id` | string | Unique project ID; default project is `"default"` |
| `name` | string | Display name |
| `project_dir` | string | Working directory absolute path; default project is `""` |
| `pinned` | boolean | Whether pinned |
| `pin_order` | integer | Pin order, lower comes first; 0 when not pinned |
| `is_default` | boolean | Whether default project |
| `hidden` | boolean | Whether soft-deleted; default project is always false |
| `session_count` | integer | Number of **non-pinned** sessions in this project |
| `last_message_at` | number \| null | Latest message timestamp (UTC seconds) in this project; null if no sessions |
| `last_user_message_at` | number \| null | Latest user message timestamp (UTC seconds); falls back to `created_at` when no user messages |
| `created_at` | number | Creation timestamp (UTC seconds); default project is 0 |

### SessionInfo

Returned by `project.get_sessions` / `project.pinned_sessions`. Projected from `metadata.json`, excluding internal fields like `delivery_context` / `channel_metadata`.

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `session_id` | string | Unique session ID |
| `title` | string | Session title |
| `created_at` | number | Creation timestamp (UTC seconds) |
| `last_message_at` | number | Last message timestamp (UTC seconds) |
| `message_count` | integer | Message count |
| `mode` | string | Session mode |
| `pinned` | boolean | Whether pinned |
| `pin_order` | integer | Pin order; 0 when not pinned |
| `project_id` | string | Bound project ID; empty string for default project |
| `project_dir` | string | Bound project path; empty string for default project |
| `last_user_message_at` | number \| null | Last user message timestamp (UTC seconds); falls back to `created_at` when no user messages |
| `model` | string | Current model identifier; empty string when unset |

### Mode Values

| Value | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `agent.plan` | Agent planning mode |
| `agent.fast` | Agent fast mode |
| `code.plan` | Code planning mode |
| `code.normal` | Code normal mode (default) |
| `code.team` | Code team mode |
| `team` | Team mode |

---

## 3. Sorting Rules

| Scenario | Rule |
|----------|------|
| Pinned sessions (`project.pinned_sessions`) | Sorted by `pin_order` ascending, detached from project groups |
| Project list (`project.list`) | Pinned projects first (by `pin_order` ascending), then by `last_user_message_at` descending, default project last |
| Sessions in project (`project.get_sessions`) | Non-pinned only, sorted by `last_user_message_at` descending |

> **Statistics**: `session_count` and `project.get_sessions` always count only **non-pinned** sessions in the current project. Pinned sessions are returned independently by `project.pinned_sessions` and are not counted in any project's `session_count`.

---

## 4. Session Interfaces

### session.list — List sessions

Flat paginated session list, unchanged behavior.

**Request params:**

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `limit` | integer | no | Return limit, default 20, max 200 |
| `offset` | integer | no | Offset, default 0 |

**Response payload:**

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `sessions` | object[] | Session metadata list (full fields) |
| `total` | integer | Total session count |
| `limit` | integer | Actual limit used |
| `offset` | integer | Actual offset used |

---

### session.create — Create session

Adds optional `project_dir` and `project_id` params; behavior is fully backward-compatible when omitted.

**Request params:**

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `session_id` | string | yes | Session ID, generated by frontend (`sess_` prefix) |
| `title` | string | no | Session title |
| `mode` | string | no | Mode, default `unknown` (overwritten with the real mode on first `chat.send`) |
| `project_dir` | string | no | Working directory absolute path. Cannot be provided alone — a valid `project_id` (non-empty, not `"default"`) must accompany it, otherwise `BAD_REQUEST`. When `project_id` is given without `project_dir`, it is auto-filled from the project's bound path |
| `project_id` | string | no | Target project ID, immutable after first bind. When non-empty and not `"default"`, must correspond to an existing visible project, otherwise `NOT_FOUND`. If `project_dir` is also provided, it must match the project's bound path (consistency check), otherwise `BAD_REQUEST`. Omitted or `"default"` (with empty `project_dir`) means the default empty project |

> Validation — `project_id` / `project_dir` binding rules:
> - Both empty (or `project_id="default"` with empty `project_dir`) → default project (backward compatible);
> - `project_id` only (no `project_dir`) → `project_dir` auto-filled from the project record;
> - `project_id` + `project_dir` both provided → consistency check (must match the project's bound path, trailing-separator/case differences tolerated);
> - `project_dir` only without a valid `project_id` → rejected (`BAD_REQUEST`).
>
> Additionally, `project_dir` non-empty must be an absolute path (`BAD_REQUEST` otherwise). Both fields are written to session metadata on creation and are immutable thereafter.

**Response payload:**

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `session_id` | string | Created session ID |

**Error codes:** `BAD_REQUEST` (`project_dir` non-empty but not absolute; `project_dir` provided without a valid `project_id`; `project_dir` does not match the project's bound path), `ALREADY_EXISTS` (session already exists), `NOT_FOUND` (`project_id` does not match an existing visible project)

---

### session.delete — Delete session

Deletes the session directory and runtime state, unchanged behavior.

**Request params:**

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `session_id` | string | yes | Target session ID |

**Response payload:**

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `session_id` | string | Deleted session ID |

**Error codes:** `NOT_FOUND` (session not found)

---

### session.rename — Rename session

Supports three semantics: query / set / clear.

**Request params:**

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `session_id` | string | yes | Target session ID |
| `title` | string | no | Omitted (or `null`) → query current title; empty/whitespace → clear title; non-empty → set (truncated to 200 chars) |

**Response payload:**

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `session_id` | string | Target session ID |
| `title` | string | Title after operation |
| `previous_title` | string | Title before operation (same as `title` for query semantics) |

> If session metadata does not exist, it is automatically created before writing; no error.

**Error codes:** `BAD_REQUEST` (`session_id` is empty)

---

### session.pin — Pin/unpin session

Pinning detaches the session from its project group into the global pinned area; unpinning returns it to the original project. All pinned sessions are renumbered compactly to 1..N after the operation.

**Request params:**

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `session_id` | string | yes | Session ID |
| `pinned` | boolean | yes | `true`=pin, `false`=unpin |

**Response payload:**

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `pinned` | boolean | Pinned state after operation |
| `pin_order` | integer | `pin_order` after operation |

> **Idempotent**: operating on a session already in the target state is treated as success.
> A newly pinned session has `pin_order` defaulting to 0, placing it at the top after renumbering.

**Error codes:** `NOT_FOUND` (session not found)

---

## 5. Project Interfaces

### project.list — List projects

Returns project list (with statistics), sorted, including the default project.

**Request params:**

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `filter` | string | no | `"all"` (default) / `"pinned"` / `"unpinned"` |
| `include_hidden` | boolean | no | Whether to include soft-deleted projects, default `false`. Only effective for `"all"` / `"unpinned"` |

**filter semantics:**

| Value | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `"all"` | Pinned projects first (by `pin_order` ascending), then by `last_user_message_at` descending, default project last |
| `"pinned"` | Pinned projects only, by `pin_order` ascending |
| `"unpinned"` | Non-pinned projects only, by `last_user_message_at` descending, default project last |

**Response payload:**

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `projects` | ProjectInfo[] | Project list, sorted |

**Error codes:** none

---

### project.get_sessions — Get sessions in project

Returns **non-pinned** sessions for the given `project_id`, sorted. Sessions are matched by `project_id` only. Passing `"default"` returns non-pinned sessions not belonging to any visible project (including sessions with empty `project_id` or matching hidden projects).

**Request params:**

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `project_id` | string | yes | Project ID; pass `"default"` for default project sessions |
| `limit` | integer | no | Return limit, default unlimited |
| `offset` | integer | no | Offset, default 0 |

**Response payload:**

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `sessions` | SessionInfo[] | Sorted non-pinned session list (by `last_user_message_at` descending) |
| `total` | integer | Total non-pinned session count in this project (before truncation) |

**Error codes:** `NOT_FOUND` (project not found or soft-deleted/hidden)

---

### project.create — Create project

Creates a project with an optional working directory. If the non-empty `project_dir` matches a hidden project, it is automatically restored. Supports creating empty-path projects (multiple allowed, distinguished by `project_id` + `name`).

**Request params:**

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `name` | string | yes | Project display name (non-empty, globally unique including hidden projects); used to update the name when restoring |
| `project_dir` | string | no | Working directory absolute path; omitted or empty creates an empty-path project |

**Response payload:**

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `project_id` | string | Project ID (`proj_<8-char hex>` for new, original ID for restored) |
| `project_dir` | string | Project path (empty string for empty-path projects) |
| `restored` | boolean | `true`=restored a hidden project, `false`=newly created |

> **Auto-restore**: when a non-empty `project_dir` matches a hidden project, sets `hidden:false` and updates the display name; its sessions automatically re-associate via `project_id`. Returns `CONFLICT` when `project_dir` duplicates an existing visible project, or `name` duplicates any existing project (including hidden).

**Error codes:** `BAD_REQUEST` (`name` empty, `project_dir` non-empty but not absolute), `CONFLICT` (`project_dir` duplicates an existing visible project, or `name` duplicates an existing project)

---

### project.rename — Rename project

Changes only the display name; does not alter the working directory path. Returns `CONFLICT` if `name` duplicates any existing project (including hidden).

**Request params:**

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `project_id` | string | yes | Project ID |
| `name` | string | yes | New name (non-empty, globally unique including hidden projects) |

**Response payload:** empty (`{}`)

**Error codes:** `BAD_REQUEST` (`name` empty), `NOT_FOUND` (project not found), `FORBIDDEN` (default project cannot be renamed), `CONFLICT` (`name` duplicates an existing project)

---

### project.pin — Pin/unpin project

Toggles project pin state. All pinned projects are renumbered compactly to 1..N after the operation.

**Request params:**

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `project_id` | string | yes | Project ID |
| `pinned` | boolean | yes | `true`=pin, `false`=unpin |

**Response payload:**

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `pinned` | boolean | Pinned state after operation |
| `pin_order` | integer | `pin_order` after operation |

> **Idempotent**: operating on a project already in the target state is treated as success.
> A newly pinned project has `pin_order` defaulting to 0, placing it at the top after renumbering.

**Error codes:** `NOT_FOUND` (project not found or hidden), `FORBIDDEN` (default project cannot be pinned)

---

### project.remove — Remove project (soft delete)

Marks the project as `hidden:true` and automatically unpins it. Its non-pinned sessions temporarily move to the default project; pinned sessions are unaffected.

**Request params:**

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `project_id` | string | yes | Project ID |

**Response payload:**

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `affected_sessions` | integer | Number of non-pinned sessions affected (temporarily moved to default project) |

> **Idempotent**: removing an already-hidden project returns `affected_sessions: 0`.

**Error codes:** `NOT_FOUND` (project not found), `FORBIDDEN` (default project cannot be removed)

---

### project.restore — Restore project

Restores a soft-deleted (`hidden:true`) project to visible. Its sessions automatically re-associate because `project_id` still matches. Returns `CONFLICT` if the restored `name` would duplicate an existing project (including hidden).

**Request params:**

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `project_id` | string | yes | Project ID |

**Response payload:**

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `affected_sessions` | integer | Number of non-pinned sessions re-associated to this project |

**Error codes:** `NOT_FOUND` (project not found), `CONFLICT` (project is not hidden — already visible, or restored `name` duplicates an existing project), `FORBIDDEN` (default project)

---

### project.pinned_sessions — Get all pinned sessions

Returns all pinned sessions, sorted by `pin_order` ascending. Pinned sessions are detached from project groups and fetched via this interface.

**Request params:** none

**Response payload:**

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `sessions` | SessionInfo[] | All pinned sessions, by `pin_order` ascending. `project_dir` still points to the original project |

> After a project is soft-deleted, its pinned sessions remain in the global pinned list, unaffected by the project's hidden state.

**Error codes:** none

---

## 6. Interface Overview

| Method | Category | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|
| `session.list` | Session | Flat pagination, unchanged |
| `session.create` | Session | Adds optional `project_dir` / `project_id` params, backward-compatible default |
| `session.delete` | Session | Unchanged |
| `session.rename` | Session | Web local handler, query/set/clear |
| `session.pin` | Session | Pin/unpin session |
| `project.list` | Project | Project list, supports filter by pin state |
| `project.get_sessions` | Project | Returns non-pinned sessions by project_id |
| `project.create` | Project | Create project (supports empty path); auto-restore on hidden path match; globally unique name |
| `project.rename` | Project | Rename |
| `project.pin` | Project | Pin/unpin project |
| `project.remove` | Project | Soft delete (hide) |
| `project.restore` | Project | Restore soft-deleted |
| `project.pinned_sessions` | Project | Get all pinned sessions, sorted by pin_order |
